concepts.title: Differentiability implies Continuity
concepts.description: A function is continuous at a point if it is differentiable at that point. However, the converse is not always true. A function can be continuous at a point without being differentiable there.

concepts.title: Geometrical Interpretation of Continuity
concepts.description: A function f(x) is continuous at x=a if the graph of f(x) is a continuous curve at x=a, i.e., the graph can be drawn without lifting the pencil from the paper.

concepts.title: Cauchy's Generalized Mean Value Theorem
concepts.description: It states that if f(x) and g(x) are continuous functions on [a,b] and differentiable on (a,b), then there exists a point c in (a,b) such that (f(b) - f(a))/(g(b) - g(a)) = (f'(c))/g'(c).

concepts.title: Product Rule Mnemonic
concepts.description: The product rule for differentiation is: d/dx (f(x)*g(x)) = f(x)*d/dx (g(x)) + g(x)*d/dx (f(x)). To remember this, use the phrase: 'First function times derivative of second, plus second function times derivative of first'.

concepts.title: Differentiating a Constant
concepts.description: Students often forget that the derivative of a constant is zero. For example, if f(x) = x^2 + 3, then f'(x) = 2x, not 2x + 3.

concepts.title: Derivatives of Trigonometric Functions
concepts.description: The derivatives of sin(x), cos(x), tan(x), cot(x), sec(x), and cosec(x) are cos(x), -sin(x), sec^2(x), -cosec^2(x), sec(x)tan(x), and -cosec(x)cot(x) respectively.

concepts.title: Differentiability at a Point
concepts.description: A function f(x) is differentiable at a point x=a if the limit of [f(x) - f(a)]/(x-a) as x approaches a exists.

concepts.title: Implicit Function Theorem
concepts.description: It states that if f(x,y) is a function of two variables and f(a,b) = 0, then there exists a function y = g(x) such that f(g(x),x) = 0 for all x in a neighbourhood of a.
